codeReview.summary: The implementation satisfies the feature requirements: all six disciplines are present with required originator attributions, synthesis is described as unified perception, via negativa as via positiva is explicitly explained, and audience fit/non-fit framing is stated plainly. Diff scope is appropriate and limited to content updates in src/pages/approach.astro.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The content-worker procedure says to write tests first, but this repo has no standalone test suite and services.yaml maps test to build. This led to an unnecessary npm run test attempt before checking mission service commands.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: .factory/skills/content-worker/SKILL.md (Work Procedure step 5) vs transcript skeleton for session ed92ed6e-2f04-4192-869e-2f12c82e1d64 (Execute npm run test, then cat .factory/services.yaml, then npm run build); .factory/services.yaml sets commands.test to npm run build

sharedStateObservations.observation: The phrase 'discipline-description component pattern' is ambiguous in shared mission context; the actual pattern is CSS class based (discipline-block/header/originator) rather than a reusable Astro component, which caused avoidable lookup attempts.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Feature description for page-approach references a component pattern; transcript skeleton shows attempts to read src/components/DisciplineDescription.astro and list src/components (directory missing); src/styles/global.css:543-593 defines the discipline-block pattern

summary: Pass. The page-approach commit is correctly scoped and meets required behavior for disciplines, synthesis, via negativa/positiva framing, and audience positioning; no code defects were identified in the reviewed implementation.
